steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests pronoun-antecedent agreement. A participial phrase opens the sentence, so the blank must be the subject of the main clause — a pronoun that refers back to the singular noun "the song" from the previous sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — those: plural demonstrative pronoun; violates number agreement with singular antecedent "the song." ✗
  • Choice B — they: third-person plural pronoun; violates number agreement with singular antecedent "the song." ✗
  • Choice C — each: refers to individual members of a group, not to a single named noun; does not function as a pronoun for "the song." ✗
  • Choice D — it: singular third-person pronoun; correctly agrees in number with singular antecedent "the song." ✓
That confirms D is correct.
